opportunities.title: FEMA Building Resilient Infrastructure and Communities (BRIC) FY2024/25
opportunities.one_liner: Major pre-disaster federal grant for shovel-ready infrastructure projects that reduce flood and hazard risk.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: FEMA has published the FY2024/25 BRIC funding opportunity, making $1 billion available to states, local governments, territories, and Tribal Nations to take proactive steps to protect communities from disasters like floods, fires, and hurricanes. BRIC provides funding for public infrastructure projects and mitigation efforts that bolster flood resilience before a disaster strikes, covering FY2024 and FY2025 with special emphasis on shovel-ready construction projects and adoption of hazard-resistant building codes. The application period is open for 120 days and the deadline for submissions is July 23, 2026. Federal funding is available for up to 75% of eligible activity costs; small, impoverished communities may be eligible for up to 90% federal cost share.
opportunities.eligibility: U.S. states, territories, federally recognized tribal governments, and local governments (as sub-applicants through states)
opportunities.budget: $1 billion total; max $20M federal share per project (National Competition); 75% federal cost share (up to 90% for impoverished communities)

opportunities.title: FEMA Flood Mitigation Assistance (FMA) Grant Program FY2024
opportunities.one_liner: Competitive federal grant to reduce or eliminate repetitive flood damage to buildings insured under the National Flood Insurance Program.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: Flood Mitigation Assistance (FMA) competitive selections focus on reducing or eliminating the risk of repetitive flood damage to buildings and structures insured by the National Flood Insurance Program (NFIP) within participating communities. On April 22, 2026, FEMA announced more than $235 million in FMA awards; for fiscal year 2024, up to $600 million is available to states, local communities, Tribal Nations, and territories. On April 30, 2026, FEMA published an amendment to the funding opportunity that incorporates recent federal guidance and aligns with overarching national goals. Applicants have until 3 p.m. ET on Thursday, August 6, 2026, to submit or revise and re-submit their application to FEMA.
opportunities.eligibility: U.S. states, territories, federally recognized Tribal Nations, and local governments (NFIP-participating communities)

opportunities.title: NFWF National Coastal Resilience Fund (NCRF) 2026
opportunities.funder: National Fish and Wildlife Foundation (NFWF), in partnership with NOAA
opportunities.one_liner: Leading U.S. fund for nature-based coastal resilience projects that protect communities from storms and flooding while restoring fish and wildlife habitat.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: NFWF's 2026 NCRF RFP makes investments in planning, design, and implementation of nature-based solutions to enhance protection for coastal communities from the impacts of storms, floods, and other natural hazards while improving habitats for fish and wildlife. Nature-based solutions include natural and hybrid (green-gray) approaches such as restoring coastal marshes, reconnecting floodplains, rebuilding dunes, and installing living shorelines. Invited applicants must submit full proposals through NFWF's Easygrants portal by June 24, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET, with award announcements expected in late November or early December 2026. Eligible applicants include nonprofit 501(c) organizations, state and territorial government agencies, local and municipal governments, Tribal governments, educational institutions, and commercial organizations.
opportunities.eligibility: Nonprofits 501(c), state/territorial/local/municipal/Tribal governments, educational institutions, and for-profit organizations in U.S. coastal states, Great Lakes states, territories, and Tribal lands (invited applicants only at full proposal stage)

opportunities.title: NSF Confronting Hazards, Impacts and Risks for a Resilient Planet (CHIRRP)
opportunities.one_liner: NSF's flagship convergence-research program for community-partnered Earth system hazard science, including flood risk and resilience.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The CHIRRP program invites projects focusing on innovative and transformative research that advances Earth system hazard knowledge and risk mitigation in partnership with affected communities. CHIRRP projects must demonstrate convergence of three essential elements: Equitable Community Partnerships; an Earth System Science approach to advance knowledge of hazards, impacts, and risks; and Actionable Solutions that increase resilience. The program currently supports planning, conference, RCN, EAGER, and RAISE proposals that catalyze ideas and support initial conceptualization aimed at formulating plans for future large-scale projects. Flood hazards — both coastal and inland — are explicitly covered within the Earth system hazard scope.
opportunities.eligibility: U.S. universities, research institutions, nonprofits, and their community partners
opportunities.budget: Varies by proposal type (EAGER up to $300K; larger RCN/RAISE/planning grants vary)

opportunities.title: EPA Sewer Overflow and Stormwater Reuse Municipal Grants (OSG Program)
opportunities.funder: U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), administered through states
opportunities.one_liner: EPA program funding green and gray infrastructure to reduce stormwater runoff, sewer overflows, and urban flooding.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The EPA's Sewer Overflow and Stormwater Reuse Municipal Grants Program helps communities and states reduce water pollution caused by stormwater and sewer runoff, funding green and gray infrastructure projects that strengthen stormwater collection systems, improve flood resilience, and address water quality issues. The OSG program is administered by states and deadlines may differ; applicants should approach their state Clean Water State Revolving Fund program or EPA Regional Office for more information on specific deadlines. This program is particularly relevant for urban and pluvial flood risk reduction.

opportunities.title: EPA Water Infrastructure Finance and Innovation Act (WIFIA) Program
opportunities.one_liner: EPA's flexible, low-cost federal loan program for significant water and stormwater infrastructure projects that enhance community flood resilience.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The Water Infrastructure Finance and Innovation Act (WIFIA) accelerates investment in water infrastructure by offering flexible financing for significant drinking water and wastewater infrastructure projects, providing long-term loans to help communities implement critical water infrastructure at lower cost. EPA's WIFIA program has announced $6.5 billion in WIFIA funding available for water systems, with an additional $550 million available to states through the State WIFIA (SWIFIA) program. Letters of Interest are accepted on a continuous, rolling basis. Projects eligible include stormwater runoff mitigation and flood-resilient water infrastructure.
opportunities.eligibility: Water utilities, municipalities, states, and other eligible water infrastructure project sponsors
opportunities.budget: $6.5 billion in WIFIA funding; additional $550 million via State WIFIA (SWIFIA)

opportunities.title: FEMA FY2026 Pre-Disaster Mitigation (PDM) Grant Program
opportunities.one_liner: FY2026 congressionally directed pre-disaster mitigation grants primarily focused on infrastructure improvements to reduce flooding risk.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The FEMA Pre-Disaster Mitigation Grant Program funds Congressionally Directed Spending projects for state, local, tribal, and territorial governments, designed to implement cost-effective measures that reduce natural hazard risk to people and property and decrease reliance on federal funding for future disasters. In FY2026, the PDM Grant Program will award $189,713,659 to projects identified in the 2026 Department of Homeland Security Appropriations Act's Joint Explanatory Statement. The FY2026 PDM projects come from 40 states and one tribe, with most projects focused on improving infrastructure to reduce flooding risk.
opportunities.eligibility: State, local, tribal, and territorial (SLTT) governments identified in the FY2026 DHS Appropriations Act

opportunities.title: Horizon Europe EU Mission on Adaptation to Climate Change (2026 Work Programme)
opportunities.one_liner: EU's flagship mission funding research and innovation for climate resilience, including flood adaptation, in European regions and communities.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The EU Mission on Adaptation to Climate Change aims to support at least 150 European regions and communities towards climate resilience by 2030, focusing on helping regions understand climate risks, develop adaptation pathways, and implement innovative solutions to enhance resilience on the ground. Current 2026 funding opportunities include HORIZON-MISS-2026-01-CLIMA-01 (National Adaptation Hubs), HORIZON-MISS-2026-01-CLIMA-02 (Facilitating implementation of actionable solutions), and HORIZON-MISS-2026-06-CLIMA-SOIL (soil resilience to extreme weather), with a deadline of September 23, 2026 for the soil resilience call. The mission's catalogue features 65 EU-funded projects helping communities tackle floods, droughts, heatwaves, wildfires, and rising seas.
opportunities.eligibility: Research institutions, regional/local authorities, businesses, and NGOs from EU member states and associated countries

opportunities.title: Horizon Europe Cluster 3: A Disaster Resilient Society for Europe (2026 Call)
opportunities.funder: European Research Executive Agency (REA) / Horizon Europe
opportunities.one_liner: EU Horizon Europe call for disaster resilience research and innovation, including flood risk solutions, with a November 2026 deadline.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The Horizon Europe 2026 call for 'A Disaster Resilient Society for Europe' is open with proposals due by November 5, 2026, covering five topics including innovative tools for search and rescue in hazardous conditions and driving innovation uptake of disaster risk solutions. The European Commission has launched a Horizon Europe Innovation Action to accelerate the adoption and scaling of high-TRL disaster risk solutions across Europe, supporting real-world deployment of technologies that improve disaster prevention, preparedness, response, and recovery across multiple hazard types including climate-related risks. The initiative has a total budget of approximately €6 million and focuses on large-scale validation, interoperability with EU civil protection systems, and overcoming barriers to deployment.
opportunities.eligibility: Consortia of research institutions, businesses, NGOs, and public bodies from EU member states and associated countries (typically 3 or more partners from different countries)
opportunities.budget: ~€6 million per Innovation Action (varies by topic)

opportunities.title: EU LIFE Programme Climate Change Adaptation Strand (2026 Call)
opportunities.one_liner: EU LIFE programme funding implementation-focused climate adaptation projects including nature-based flood risk solutions.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The LIFE Climate Change Adaptation strand funds projects that strengthen resilience, reduce climate vulnerability, and support implementation of EU adaptation policies, with an indicative €28 million budget for 2026 that prioritises risk assessment tools, nature-based solutions, climate-proof infrastructure, and systemic policy integration. The strand supports practical, scalable solutions that improve resilience across sectors, ecosystems, and governance levels while contributing to a climate-resilient Europe by 2050. Projects must demonstrate measurable impact, avoid maladaptation, and align with the European Climate Law and EU Adaptation Strategy.
opportunities.eligibility: Public and private organisations from EU member states and associated countries
opportunities.budget: ~€28 million indicative budget for 2026 adaptation strand
